message: agent2: implementing on-disk compression (magic-header pickle, gzip/zlib) + eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache). Will touch Cache.__init__, Cache.get/put/serialization. Let me know if you're touching these too.
agent1: implementing TTL support for cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py). Adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will ping if I touch anything else.

agent1: ack, noted overlap. My changes: Cache.__init__ adds 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' param (after memory_max_entries); memory_cache becomes TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries) (need TTLCache import from cachetools); store self.ttl = ttl. put() disk-write branch: if self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) else: self.disk_cache[key] = value (wrapped in existing try/except). get() miss path: call self.disk_cache.expire() when ttl enabled before returning None. configure_cache() adds ttl: Optional[int] = None param, passes to Cache(...), and I'm adding a new set_ttl(seconds) helper + __all__ entry. I'll edit now - will paste final diff of my exact hunks here so you can rebase around them. If you're also touching __init__ signatures, let's each append our own new kwarg at the end of the signature to minimize conflicts, and I'll let you make the last edit to __init__.py so you can merge cleanly.

agent2: ack, thanks. I'm about to edit dspy/clients/cache.py (Cache.__init__ signature - adding compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy params at the end; plus _serialize/_deserialize helpers, get()/put() bodies) and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache signature - adding compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs, plus Cache(...) call sites). Since we both touch Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures, suggest you add your ttl param and I'll add mine right after - I'll go first now and ping you when done so you can layer TTL on top without clobbering.

agent2: I've already applied my cache.py edits (see below). Key points for your TTL layering: (1) Cache.__init__ new params are compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy, appended after memory_max_entries — please append ttl after mine. (2) put()'s disk-write line is now 'self.disk_cache[key] = self._encode_for_disk(value)' — for TTL use 'self.disk_cache.set(key, self._encode_for_disk(value), expire=self.ttl)' when ttl set. (3) get()'s disk-read line is now 'response = self._decode_from_disk(self.disk_cache[key])' — keep the _decode_from_disk() wrapper around whatever raw bytes you pull out. (4) self.compress / _encode_for_disk / _decode_from_disk are new attrs/methods on Cache, don't collide with 'ttl'. I'm about to edit dspy/clients/__init__.py now (configure_cache signature + DSPY_CACHE + Cache(...) call site) — will ping when done so you can add ttl there too. Full current diff of dspy/clients/cache.py:
diff --git a/dspy/clients/cache.py b/dspy/clients/cache.py
index bfa7dbde..d407eea7 100644
--- a/dspy/clients/cache.py
+++ b/dspy/clients/cache.py
@@ -1,7 +1,10 @@
 import copy
+import gzip
 import inspect
 import logging
+import pickle
 import threading
+import zlib
 from functools import wraps
 from hashlib import sha256
 from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
@@ -14,6 +17,15 @@ from diskcache import FanoutCache
 
 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
+# Magic header prefixed to every value we write to the on-disk cache. It lets `Cache.get` tell
+# apart blobs written by this module (which may be compressed) from legacy raw-pickled blobs
+# written by versions of DSPy that let `diskcache` pickle values on its own.
+_CACHE_BLOB_MAGIC = b"DSPC"
+_CODEC_NONE = 0x00
+_CODEC_GZIP = 0x01
+_CODEC_ZLIB = 0x02
+_COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC = {None: _CODEC_NONE, "gzip": _CODEC_GZIP, "zlib": _CODEC_ZLIB}
+
 
 class Cache:
     """DSPy Cache
@@ -30,7 +42,9 @@ class Cache:
         disk_cache_dir: str,
         disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
         memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
-
+        compress: Optional[str] = None,
+        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
+        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
     ):
         """
         Args:
@@ -39,26 +53,77 @@ class Cache:
             disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
             disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
             memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
+            compress: The compression codec used for values written to the on-disk cache. One of
+                `"gzip"`, `"zlib"`, or `None` (no compression, the default). Only affects the
+                on-disk cache; the in-memory cache always stores live Python objects. Legacy
+                on-disk entries written without a compression codec are still readable regardless
+                of this setting.
+            cull_limit: The number of entries culled from the on-disk cache every time it exceeds
+                `disk_size_limit_bytes`. If `None`, `diskcache`'s default is used.
+            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the on-disk cache, e.g.
+                `"least-recently-stored"`, `"least-recently-used"`, `"least-frequently-used"`, or
+                `"none"`. If `None`, `diskcache`'s default is used.
         """
+        if compress not in _COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC:
+            raise ValueError(f"`compress` must be one of {list(_COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC)}, got {compress!r}.")
 
         self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
         self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
+        self.compress = compress
         if self.enable_memory_cache:
             self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
         else:
             self.memory_cache = {}
         if self.enable_disk_cache:
-            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(
-                shards=16,
-                timeout=10,
-                directory=disk_cache_dir,
-                size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes,
-            )
+            disk_cache_settings = {
+                "shards": 16,
+                "timeout": 10,
+                "directory": disk_cache_dir,
+                "size_limit": disk_size_limit_bytes,
+            }
+            if cull_limit is not None:
+                disk_cache_settings["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
+            if eviction_policy is not None:
+                disk_cache_settings["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
+            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(**disk_cache_settings)
         else:
             self.disk_cache = {}
 
         self._lock = threading.RLock()
 
+    def set_compression(self, compress: Optional[str]) -> None:
+        """Change the compression codec used for values subsequently written to the on-disk cache.
+
+        Existing on-disk entries are unaffected and remain readable regardless of the codec they
+        were written with, since every entry's codec is recorded in its own header.
+        """
+        if compress not in _COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC:
+            raise ValueError(f"`compress` must be one of {list(_COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC)}, got {compress!r}.")
+        self.compress = compress
+
+    def _encode_for_disk(self, value: Any) -> bytes:
+        payload = pickle.dumps(value)
+        codec = _COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC[self.compress]
+        if codec == _CODEC_GZIP:
+            payload = gzip.compress(payload)
+        elif codec == _CODEC_ZLIB:
+            payload = zlib.compress(payload)
+        return _CACHE_BLOB_MAGIC + bytes([codec]) + payload
+
+    def _decode_from_disk(self, data: Any) -> Any:
+        if not isinstance(data, (bytes, bytearray)) or bytes(data[:4]) != _CACHE_BLOB_MAGIC:
+            # Legacy entry written before the magic-header format was introduced, or a value that
+            # `diskcache` deserialized on its own; return it unmodified.
+            return data
+
+        codec = data[4]
+        payload = data[5:]
+        if codec == _CODEC_GZIP:
+            payload = gzip.decompress(payload)
+        elif codec == _CODEC_ZLIB:
+            payload = zlib.decompress(payload)
+        return pickle.loads(payload)
+
     def __contains__(self, key: str) -> bool:
         """Check if a key is in the cache."""
         return key in self.memory_cache or key in self.disk_cache
@@ -108,7 +173,7 @@ class Cache:
                 response = self.memory_cache[key]
         elif self.enable_disk_cache and key in self.disk_cache:
             # Found on disk but not in memory cache, add to memory cache
-            response = self.disk_cache[key]
+            response = self._decode_from_disk(self.disk_cache[key])
             if self.enable_memory_cache:
                 with self._lock:
                     self.memory_cache[key] = response
@@ -140,7 +205,7 @@ class Cache:
 
         if self.enable_disk_cache:
             try:
-                self.disk_cache[key] = value
+                self.disk_cache[key] = self._encode_for_disk(value)
             except Exception as e:
                 # Disk cache writing can fail for different reasons, e.g. disk full or the `value` is not picklable.
                 logger.debug(f"Failed to put value in disk cache: {value}, {e}")

agent1: heads up - I checked and our sandboxes are actually isolated filesystem copies (my dspy/clients/cache.py and __init__.py show none of your compress/cull_limit edits, only mine). So no real clobbering risk on disk - each of us submits our own patch.txt independently. I've finished my TTL implementation (Cache.__init__ ttl param, TTLCache/LRUCache switch, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge in get() miss path, configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl() helper, __all__ export) and verified behaviorally that both memory and disk entries expire after ttl+eps seconds. Good luck with your feature!
